Comments: Hello Denis, first I would like to say that you're concise, honest reporting is a soothing balm to my soul. however, I find myself in this constant state of hypervigilance. Vasolating from feelings of survivors guilt and catastrophizing the worst possible outcomes. I think I speak for myself and others by saying we are all traumatized and emotionally exhausted. I don't think there are any words that could be a comfort right now. Although I wish with all my heart that there were.
From the East Coast of Florida to the west coast. Our thoughts and prayers are with you and healing hugs as well.

Need advice from Mississippi. Disney trip planned for Wednesday 9th thru Wednesday 16.
Cancel completely ? Try to shift days ?
Any advice appreciated. Completely clueless about this kind of weather. Thank you
hang tight, unless you can very easily (and cheaply) move days.

ou know that’s impossible with Disney… nothing is cheap or easy. I
Just know that I need to start cancellations soon. Not sure how long to wait

It's a bit to early to tell- it could be a weak hurricane or tropical storm, or it could go south and it may only be some wind and rain. Or it may go way south and you get nothing. Things should be more clear by Monday. Regardless, WDW is safe during storms and will only close if the eye of the storm is forecast to go over the area. If you don't mind being soaking wet, I say keep the trip. Hurricane days at Disney can be great because no one is there.
thank you for your advice.
Do you also have a dog?
we will be there. The rain will add to the pixie dust. It looks like it clears up after Wednesday. Keep the trip! We are!

I highly recommend rescheduling your trip for another time. I've been living in Florida for 45 years. There is a possibility of a MAJOR hurricane cat 3 making landfall in the Tampa Bay area on Wed. That's not very far from Disney World. The weather conditions will be poor Thursday or Friday.
thank you. We rescheduled.

While I'm not 100% sure, I *believe* Disney World will be closed on Wed and Thurs. *If* Milton makes landfall as a cat 3 MAJOR hurricane in the Tampa Bay, St Petersburg vicinity, ( 110 mph winds with 130mph wind gusts, it's expected to travel across the state along the I-4 Corridor which that means Sanford and Orlando and exit in either Volusia or Flagler county. Not exactly ideal weather for a week at Disney World. Make sure to call Disney World on Mon or Tues to inquire with them.

Don't think they expected wind gusts from Helene to top over 100-130 mph in NC.
New data out from readings on mountain tops. Clearly the predicted outcomes were underestimated.
From a meteorologist in NC:
"The flooding has gotten most of the attention but the wind damage in the high country of NC is exceptional, especially along the ridge tops. This is atop Black Balsam. Officially, the highest gust was 106 on Mt Mitchell with an unofficial gust of 131 mph on top of Beech Mountain. From Appalachian Life."

Please share this link for anyone in need of rescue from flooding, fires, or the aftermath of Hurricane Milton. It’s crucial to share before the storm hits, as communication may be disrupted afterward. We are a veteran-led nonprofit rescue group, Grey Bull Rescue, trained and ready to assist. We successfully helped during Hurricane Helene and still have crews in North Carolina. We're here to help with Milton as well.
Thanks for all you do!
